data.title: MeiraGTx rises after publication of positive data from gene therapy trial for eye disorder
data.description:
MeiraGTx (MGTX) stock rose 7% after publishing promising results from the first human trial using gene therapy to treat AIPL1-associated severe retinal dystrophy in children.

Across all trials so far, 11 out of 11 children with LCA4, a severe genetic eye disorder, have shown meaningful vision improvement. All 11 children were legally blind at birth but gained visual acuity within four weeks of treatment.

Given these results, MeiraGTx said that it plans to submit a Marketing Authorization Application under exceptional circumstances in consultation with the UK’s Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency, without requiring further clinical data.

The company said that the gene therapy has been granted orphan drug as well as rare pediatric disease designation by FDA and orphan designation by the European Commission.

data.title: Balchem projects strong 2025 growth amid new product launches and robust segment performance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Balchem Corporation (NASDAQ:BCPC) Q4 2024

Management View

  • According to Ted Harris, Chairman, President, and CEO, Balchem achieved record consolidated sales of $240 million in Q4 2024, representing 4.9% growth year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA reached $63 million with a margin of 26.2%. Consolidated net income rose by 26% to $34 million, translating into a diluted EPS of $1.03 on a GAAP basis. Adjusted net earnings were $37 million, or $1.13 per share.
  • Harris highlighted notable achievements in 2024, including record full-year sales of $954 million, strong free cash flow of $147 million, and a reduction in net debt to $140 million with a leverage ratio of 0.6 times. He emphasized strategic progress on product innovation, particularly in launching three new products in the Human Nutrition & Health segment and one in the Animal Nutrition & Health segment.
  • Harris stated, "We are excited about 2025 and believe the company is well-positioned to deliver both top and bottom-line growth while advancing key initiatives."
  • CFO Martin Bengtsson reported Q4 gross margin improvement to 36% of sales, up 320 basis points year-over-year, driven by a favorable portfolio mix. He added, "We reduced our debt by $37 million in the quarter, reflecting our continued focus on financial discipline."

Outlook

  • Harris projected momentum in the Human Nutrition & Health segment, supported by new launches like VitaCholine Pro-Flo and K2VITAL DELTA Fermented, and ongoing growth in the Animal Nutrition & Health segment driven by the dairy market's recovery and new product penetration.
  • Management reiterated confidence in sustaining year-over-year growth in 2025, emphasizing their robust product portfolio and market positioning.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 adjusted EPS was $1.13 vs. analysts' estimate of $1.10, and revenues were $240 million vs. estimates of $239.96 million.
  • Sales in the Human Nutrition & Health segment increased by 6.8% to $147 million, driven by growth in food ingredients and nutrients businesses. Adjusted earnings from operations for this segment rose by 21.8%.
  • Animal Nutrition & Health sales rose by 0.3% to $58 million, with sequential improvement attributed to a recovery in the dairy market and the launch of AminoShure-XL. Adjusted earnings for the segment grew by 6.5%.
  • Specialty Products segment revenue increased by 6% to $33 million, driven by higher sales in the Performance Gases business. Adjusted earnings for the segment climbed 11.2%.

Q&A

  • Bob Labick, CJS Securities, asked about growth drivers in Human Nutrition & Health. Ted Harris highlighted strong performance in nutrients and a return to growth in food ingredients, with encapsulated products performing particularly well. He stated, "The K2 business had a standout quarter, and encapsulated products delivered growth across all lines."
  • Ram Selvaraju, H.C. Wainwright, inquired about tariff exposure and marketing strategies for products like VitaCholine Pro-Flo. Harris noted minimal tariff risks and outlined plans for increased marketing efforts, including social media and partnerships, to promote VitaCholine's inclusion in multivitamins.
  • Daniel Harriman, Sidoti & Company, asked about margin expectations for 2025. Bengtsson indicated potential pressure from slightly inflationary raw material costs but emphasized a favorable portfolio mix supporting healthy margins.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on growth sustainability and product performance, maintaining a neutral to slightly positive tone with questions targeting strategic plans and market conditions.
  • Management displayed confidence during prepared remarks, emphasizing strong results and innovation. However, in Q&A, they adopted a slightly defensive tone on topics like tariffs and raw material costs, using phrases like "we believe" and "relatively well-positioned."
  • Compared to the prior quarter, analyst sentiment remained stable, while management maintained a consistent tone of optimism.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Compared to Q3 2024, Q4 saw continued revenue growth across all segments, with Animal Nutrition & Health returning to modest year-over-year growth after sequential improvement. Adjusted EBITDA margins increased slightly to 26.2%.
  • Management's tone remained optimistic, but there was an increased focus on mitigating external risks such as tariffs and raw material inflation.
  • Analysts' focus shifted slightly from recovery in Animal Nutrition & Health to long-term growth drivers and product innovation in Human Nutrition & Health.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential challenges from geopolitical uncertainty, tariffs, and inflationary pressures on raw materials.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the European feed market's stabilization and its impact on Animal Nutrition & Health growth, as well as potential margin pressure from portfolio mix shifts.

Final Takeaway

Balchem closed 2024 with record-breaking results and a positive outlook for 2025. Growth in Human and Animal Nutrition segments, supported by innovative product launches and healthy market conditions, positions the company well for continued success. Management remains confident but cautious, closely monitoring risks like tariffs and inflation impacts while focusing on strategic marketing and operational efficiency to sustain momentum.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: AMD could sell data center plants for as much as $4B: report
data.description:
• AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) is in discussions with several companies in Asia to sell server chip manufacturing plants, with the deal potentially being worth around $4B, including debt, Bloomberg reported.
  • AMD shares fell 2.2% in midday trading on Friday, in-line with a broader market sell off.
  • The sale, could be announced as soon as the second quarter, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. The plants are in New Jersey and Texas and Compal Electronics, Inventec, Pegatron and Wistron are among the interested parties, Bloomberg added.
  • The Dr. Lisa-Su led AMD took ownership of the plants when it acquired ZT Systems in August 2024 for $4.9B in an effort to boost its data center AI systems capabilities. At the time of the deal, AMD said Secaucus, N.J.-based ZT Systems' experience in designing and optimizing cloud computing solutions would help cloud and enterprise customers accelerate the deployment of AMD-powered AI infrastructure at scale.
  • AMD did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Mutual funds are UW the Mag 7 in Q4 citing diversification challenges
data.description:
Mutual funds are underweight the Magnificent Seven, said Ryan Hammond, vice president of Equity Strategy at Goldman Sachs, in a note.

In the fourth quarter, large-cap mutual funds were 790 basis points underweight the Magnificent Seven stocks – Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), Tesla (TSLA), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), and Meta Platforms (META) – compared to 806 basis points in the third quarter.

Tesla (TSLA), however, was the most added stock among large-cap mutual fund managers in the fourth quarter. According to Goldman, 30 funds took a new position in Tesla (TSLA), and 93 funds added to their existing positions, compared with 36 funds cutting their position, and only two funds dropping their positions altogether.

“Diversification guidelines have created challenges for many mutual fund managers when deciding their allocation to the Mag 7,” said Hammond. “These underweights weighed significantly on mutual fund performance in 2023 and 2024, when the Mag 7 outperformed the S&P 493 by 63 percentage points and 32 percentage points, respectively.”

The Magnificent Seven stocks have returned just 1% year-to-date. The rest of the S&P 500 returned 6%, and the index (SP500) in total has returned 4% year-to-date.

“The substantial mutual fund underweight in the Mag 7 has flipped from a headwind to a tailwind, with 51% of large-cap managers outperforming their benchmarks year-to-date,” said Hammond.

Here is an overview:

  • Apple (AAPL) – 294 basis points underweight by MFs; down 1.25% year-to-date
  • Nvidia (NVDA) – 159 basis points underweight by MFs; up 2% year-to-date
  • Tesla (TSLA) – 130 basis points underweight by MFs; down 15.77% year-to-date
  • Microsoft (MSFT) – 102 basis points underweight by MFs; down 1.91% year-to-date
  • Amazon (AMZN) – 49 basis points underweight by MFs; down 1.36% year-to-date
  • Alphabet (GOOGL) – 32 basis points underweight by MFs; down 4.68% year-to-date
  • Meta Platforms (META) – 25 basis points underweight by MFs; up 17.03% year-to-date

data.title: Moderna, Novavax rise as new bat coronavirus identified in China
data.description:
• Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA), Novavax (NASDAQ:NVAX), BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX), and Pfizer (PFE), which all make COVID-19 vaccines, are trading higher Friday following a report that Chinese researchers have identified a new bat coronavirus that could have the ability to transfer to humans.
  • The development was made public in a study led by Shi Zhengli, a virologist who directs the Center for Emerging Infectious Diseases at the Wuhan Institute of Virology.
  • The new research is causing concern as the new coronavirus, known as HKU5-CoV-2, uses the ACE2 receptor to infect. That same receptor is also used by SARS-CoV-2, the virus that causes COVID-19.
  • "Structural and functional analyses indicate that HKU5-CoV-2 has a better adaptation to human ACE2 than lineage 1 HKU5-CoV," the study, published in the journal Cell, states.

data.title: Rivian bulls dig in for the long haul as early signs of a transformation emerge
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) faded in afternoon trading on Friday as the initial buzz over a Q4 gross profit was overshadowed by the electric vehicle's cautious 2025 guidance, and with general headwinds seen for the next few quarters.

On Wall Street, several firms backed their bullish long-term view on Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN). Needham reiterated its Buy rating and boosted its target to $17. "We continue to see RIVN as a long-term winner in the ICE to EV transition given owner satisfaction and passion ahead of launching the more affordable, TAM increasing R2 vehicle, combined with RIVN's strong balance sheet post the Volkswagen joint venture," updated analyst Chris Pierce. He noted that bulls can take solace in stronger than expected Q4 gross margins and balance sheet improvement, with bears focusing on 2025 delivery guidance and a slow start to the year given Q1 messaging.

Wedbush Securities also said it remains confident in the long-term Rivian (RIVN) vision that is in the midst of massive transformation, with the company looking to optimize its R1 production and preparing to ramp its R2 and midsize platform supply chains. The maintained its Outperform rating and price target of $20.

Shares of Rivian (RIVN) were down 2.5% in afternoon trading.

data.title: Trucking stocks hit the brakes on possible Amazon LTL expansion
data.description:
Trucking stocks are losing traction on Friday after J.P. Morgan warned that the less-than-truckload (LTL) sector could lose valuable market share if Amazon (AMZN) enters as a "for-hire" competitor, posing a threat to established players like Old Dominion (NASDAQ:ODFL), Saia (SAIA), and Knight-Swift Transportation (KNX), among others.

The sector is also on edge following comments from TFI International (TFII) CEO Alain Bedard during the company’s earnings call. After reporting a weak Q4 and a cautious Q1 outlook, Bedard cautioned that the freight industry is in a "deep recession," with volumes still alarmingly low.

“It’s still a very, very difficult environment,” Bedard said on the call. “I would probably say the first 6 months of 2025 will be more difficult than the first 6 months of 2024.”

Bedard's guarded sentiment was shared by other major players in the LTL industry during the course of Q4 earnings. While most see signs of improvement in the industry, specifically targeting the second half of the year, the LTL segment will continue to face volume headwinds for the first two quarters of 2025 given both seasonal and macroeconomic factors. This outlook was likely amplified by the purchasing managers’ composite index (“PMI”) for February, which fell to a 17-month low.

“Companies report widespread concerns about the impact of federal government policies, ranging from spending cuts to tariffs and geopolitical developments,” the chief economist at S&P Global said in regard to the report.

Amidst the challenges facing LTL shippers, Amazon (AMZN) plans to build up its LTL capacity.

“The most obvious risk for LTL stocks is if this service launches for external users at some point in 2026 as it is pretty much impossible to put that disruptive idea back in the box,” J.P. Morgan’s Brian Ossenbeck said in his research note.

Amazon does have a significant number of cross-docks and assets from which to build an internal LTL network, Ossenbeck says, adding that an Amazon build-up will actually create less demand for rivals while at the same time create more demand for truckload.

“Overall, we will remain watchful for Amazon Freight building up its internal LTL capacity before potentially entering the market as a for-hire carrier…to which the impact on sentiment would be negative and outsized even if [Amazon] might not easily scale up to be a credible risk to other carriers,” Ossenbeck says.

Among trucking stocks, Saia (SAIA), Old Dominion (NASDAQ:ODFL) and XPO (XPO) are taking the brunt of Friday’s selling pressure, all three of which are down by double-digits. Shares of ArcBest (ARCB), Knight-Swift Transportation (KNX), and RXO (RXO) are also taking on heavy losses of 6% to 9%.

data.title: ConocoPhillips to sell interests Ursa, Europa fields to Shell in $735M deal
data.description:
ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) said Friday it agreed to sell its interests in the Ursa and Europa fields and Ursa Oil Pipeline Company to Shell (SHEL) for $735M.

FY 2024 production associated with ConocoPhillips' (NYSE:COP) 15.96% interest in the Ursa field and 1% interest in the Europa field totaled ~8K boe/day.

Concoo (COP) has been trying to offload non-core assets to help reduce its debt after last year's $22.5B takeover of Marathon Oil.

"This transaction reflects our ongoing commitment to further strengthen our portfolio by divesting non-core assets and shows significant progress toward our $2B disposition target," the company said.

data.title: Select food stocks defy broad market selling pressure as investors turn defensive
data.description:
Food stocks outperformed on Friday amid some broad selling pressure across the stock market.

Investors were seemed to be focused on some dour economic reports. The February S&P Composite PMI fell to 51.6 from the 52.7 prior print. The service index dropped to 49.7 from 53.0. U.S. business activity growth was highlighted as being close to stalling in February, as a renewed fall in services output offset faster manufacturing growth.

“The upbeat mood seen among U.S. businesses at the start of the year has evaporated, replaced with a darkening picture of heightened uncertainty,” Chris Williamson, chief business economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence, said in the report.

The food sector has also seen some positive commentary from major players on volume trends stabilizing after a multi-year period of relying on pricing for sales growth.

Notable gainers of more than 2% in Friday trading included Hain Celestial (HAIN), Hershey (HSY), Utz Brands (UTZ), J&J Snack Foods (JJSF), Conagra Brands (CAG), General Mills (GIS), J.M. Smucker (SJM), Kraft Heinz (KHC), B&G Foods (BGS), Farmer Bros. (FARM), and Campbell's Co. (CPB).

data.title: Mirum gains FDA approval of chenodiol for rare lipid storage disease
data.description:
• The U.S. FDA has approved Mirum Pharmaceuticals' (NASDAQ:MIRM) Ctexli (chenodiol), the first-ever treatment for cerebrotendinous xanthomatosis (CTX), a rare lipid storage condition.
  • The disease is a genetic metabolic disorder caused by a mutation in a gene that leads to a deficiency of the enzyme the body needs to break down fats. Because of reduced bile acid production in the liver, those with CTX can't break down cholesterol normally, resulting in the deposit of atypical cholesterol metabolites in the brain, liver, skin and tendons.
  • Approval was based on results of a 24-week double-blind, placebo controlled, randomized crossover withdrawal trial. Those in the active drug arm -- given Ctexli 250 mg three times daily -- saw significant reduction in plasma cholestanol and urine 23S-pentol compared to those on placebo.
  • Ctexli's labeling includes a warning for liver toxicity with increased risk for liver damage in patients with pre-existing liver disease or bile duct abnormalities.
